title: Life Biosciences Closes $80M Series D to Fund First Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, closed a fully-subscribed $80M Series D to fund its Phase 1 trial of ER-100 for optic neuropathies. This makes it the first company to reach human trials with partial epigenetic reprogramming (OSK factors), a landmark moment for the cellular rejuvenation field. Q1 2026 saw $3.7B deployed across 49 longevity deals, a 56% increase over Q1 2025.

title: Longevity Biotech Rebounds: Broad Rally Across Healthcare and Genomics ETFs
description: After a broad selloff on Friday April 10, longevity and biotech ETFs staged a strong Monday rebound. IDNA led gains at +3.8%, SBIO rose +2.8%, XBI +2.5%, and ARKG +2.4%. The rally suggests renewed investor confidence in the healthcare and genomics sectors amid stabilizing macro conditions. Only THNR (weight-loss drug ETF) remained flat.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Major Multi-Drug Aging Trial: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and SGLT2i
description: UT Health San Antonio is preparing one of the largest human aging trials, testing rapamycin, semaglutide, and dapagliflozin to determine if existing FDA-approved drugs can slow biological aging. The NIH-funded study will enroll over 700 participants and represents a critical step in translating decades of animal longevity research into human evidence. ARPA-H has committed $144M to the PROSPR program supporting this work.

title: SASP Score Blood Test and Retrotransposon Therapy: Two New Aging Biomarker Breakthroughs
description: Fight Aging reports two significant developments: a composite SASP Score derived from blood proteomics using UK Biobank data and deep learning that predicts mortality and chronic disease, and a proof-of-concept study showing the antiretroviral FTC/TAF reduces epigenetic age clocks (DunedinPACE -0.061, PhenoAge -6.33 years), supporting retrotransposon suppression as a gerotherapeutic strategy.

title: Merck Bets $838M on AI-Driven Antibody Discovery With Infinimmune
description: Merck signed an $838M deal with Infinimmune, a startup using AI to analyze human immune cell libraries and discover novel antibodies. The deal signals Big Pharma's growing appetite for AI-native drug discovery platforms, with implications for longevity-adjacent targets in autoimmune and inflammatory aging conditions. Longevity.Technology noted this as evidence that Big Pharma is seeking faster, more human-first approaches to age-related medicine.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80M Series D for Cellular Rejuvenation Therapies
description: Boston-based Life Biosciences closed an $80M Series D to fund its Phase 1 trial of ER-100, an AAV-delivered OSK gene therapy for optic neuropathies, and extend operations into 2027.

title: Longevity Biotech Sector Hits $3.7B in Q1 2026 Funding Across 49 Deals
description: Q1 2026 saw approximately $3.7B deployed across 49 longevity biotech deals, a 56% increase over Q1 2025, with an average deal size of $91.2M.

title: US Longevity Gains More Widespread Than Previously Thought, BMJ Open Study Finds
description: A University of Wisconsin study published in BMJ Open challenges prior estimates of stagnating US longevity, finding gains across all states for birth cohorts 1941-2000.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Study
description: NIH-funded randomized placebo-controlled trial of rapamycin in 84 older adults will evaluate dosing, safety, and biological markers to establish evidence-based guidance for the drug's use in aging.

title: Over 700 South Texans to Join Major Trial Testing Rapamycin, Semaglutide, and Dapagliflozin for Aging
description: UT Health San Antonio prepares one of the largest human aging trials, testing three existing drugs to determine if they can slow biological processes behind aging.

title: Merck Partners With Infinimmune in $838M AI Antibody Discovery Deal
description: Merck taps Infinimmune's AI-driven platform to discover novel antibodies, in a deal worth up to $838M in upfront and milestone payments.

title: Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Science Narrows Toward Biomarker-Guided Approaches
description: A comprehensive review finds human proof-of-biology exists for senolytics, but the field is splitting into academic first-gen, biotech pipeline, and local delivery lanes. Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents.

title: USPTO Publishes AI-Driven Senolytic Compound Patent Screening 800K+ Molecules
description: Patent application US20260097022A1 discloses AI neural networks trained on 2,352 compounds to predict senolytic activity across 800K+ molecules, with compounds binding Bcl-2 proteins to reduce senescent cell burden.

title: ARPA-H Commits $144M to PROSPR Program for Longevity Research
description: The Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health allocates up to $144M to support studies aimed at slowing biological aging and extending quality years of life, with UT Health San Antonio playing a leading role.

title: Bryan Johnson Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA to 2026 Blueprint Stack
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 supplement update includes taking NMN/NR six days per week instead of seven, removing rapamycin (stopped in 2024), and adding low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain function and cellular support.

title: Noom Acquires Compounding Pharmacy to Dispense GLP-1s and Longevity Peptides
description: Noom acquired a 503A compounding pharmacy to dispense GLP-1s, peptides like sermorelin, and NAD+ alongside behavioral coaching for healthy aging.

title: Annovis Advances Buntanetap Into Phase 3 for Alzheimer's and Parkinson's
description: Annovis Bio advances buntanetap, an oral molecule reducing amyloid beta, tau, and alpha-synuclein translation, into Phase 3 trials for both Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease.

title: Longevity Biotech Crosses From Thesis to Mainstream in 2026
description: Alex Zhavoronkov (Insilico Medicine) argues 2026 is the breakout year for longevity biotech, with real translational science, clearer clinical pathways, and investors treating aging biology as a serious opportunity.

title: NOVOS Core Clinical Trial Shows Cardiovascular Aging Benefits in Placebo-Controlled Study
description: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial shows NOVOS Core improves endothelial function, arterial flexibility, and blood flow in adults 40+, with 2.9% sustained FMD improvement.

title: Antiretroviral FTC/TAF Reduces Epigenetic Age Clocks: First Retrotransposon Gerotherapeutic Proof
description: Study shows the antiretroviral FTC/TAF reduces DunedinPACE by -0.061 and PhenoAge by -6.33 years in healthy adults, providing first human proof-of-concept for retrotransposon-targeted anti-aging therapy.

title: SASP Score From Blood Proteomics Predicts Mortality and Chronic Disease Using Deep Learning
description: New composite SASP Score from UK Biobank blood proteomics data using deep learning validates as a predictor of mortality and chronic conditions; multimodal exercise alters its trajectory.

title: LAMP1 Surface Marker Identifies Senescent Cells for Targeted Elimination
description: Study identifies aberrant localization of LAMP1 protein on the surface of senescent cells, enabling targeted elimination of aged cells showing clear senescent phenotypes.

title: T Cells Effectively Target Senescent Cells in Mouse Pulmonary Fibrosis Model
description: Lifespan Research Institute scientists discovered that a subset of T cells effectively targets senescent cells and improves outcomes in a mouse model of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress Shifts Focus From 'Fixing Aging' to Preserving Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin reframes aging as progressive loss of coordination between biological systems rather than a single defect to fix.

title: ENDO12 Compound Blocks Munc13-4-Syntaxin 7 to Reduce Inflammation Without Impairing Antiviral Response
description: Researchers screened 32,000 compounds and identified ENDO12, which blocks the Munc13-4-syntaxin 7 interaction to reduce autoimmune inflammation while preserving antiviral immunity.

title: Top Longevity Startups 2026: Over $13B in Venture Funding With Altos Labs Leading at $3B
description: The longevity market has attracted over $13B in venture funding, with Altos Labs ($3B), Retro Biosciences ($1.2B), and Oura ($1.2B) leading. The top 10 startups capture 67% of all funding.

title: Rapamycin Review in Frontiers in Aging: Promising but Standardized Biomarkers Still Needed
description: Comprehensive review of rapamycin for longevity finds that while preclinical data is compelling, the absence of standardized pharmacodynamic biomarkers makes it difficult to confirm efficacy in humans.

title: Peloton Named Official Movement Partner for Longevity Show 2026 in London
description: Peloton will provide rooftop workouts, 5K runs, and fitness events at the Longevity Show 2026 (June 26-27, London), bridging the fitness and longevity communities.

title: On Aging 2026 Conference: Largest US Multidisciplinary Aging Event Opens April 20 in Atlanta
description: The ASA's On Aging 2026 conference takes place April 20-23 at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta, themed 'The Power of Belonging' and focusing on amplifying voices and advancing justice in aging.

title: Longevity Sector Sees $23.2B Market in 2026, Projected to Reach $34.8B by 2030
description: The longevity biotech sector stands at $23.2B in 2026 and is projected to reach $34.82B by 2030 at an 11% CAGR, driven by record Q1 funding and expanding clinical pipelines.
